description_long: This is a rugged, well-worn hammer with a straight, cylindrical handle and a robust, rusted metal head. The handle appears to be made of dark, weathered wood, showing signs of heavy use and exposure to the elements. The head is a cross-peen type, featuring a flat striking face on one side and a pointed, wedge-like peen on the other, both heavily oxidized and pitted with rust. The overall construction is utilitarian, with no visible branding or decorative elements. The hammer's surfaces are rough and matte, with clear evidence of age and repeated impact. This tool would be commonly found in urban repair settings, garages, or construction sites.
description: A heavily used, rusted cross-peen hammer with a dark wooden handle and a corroded metal head, showing significant wear and age.
description_view_0: The hammer is seen at an angle, clearly displaying the full length of the handle and both the flat and pointed faces of the head.
description_view_1: Viewed from the end of the handle, only the narrow profile of the hammer head is visible, emphasizing its thickness.
description_view_2: The opposite side angle shows the pointed peen and the handle, highlighting the rust and wear on the head.
description_view_3: A direct side view of the hammer head, showing its rectangular cross-section and the extent of surface corrosion.
attribute_handle_type: straight wooden handle, cylindrical, dark and weathered, no visible grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: cross-peen hammer head, one flat striking face and one pointed wedge peen, heavily rusted
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: significant rust, pitting, and surface wear on the head; handle appears worn and aged
